articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: United States men beat Costa Rica as Agyemang strikes again
article.description: The United States beat Costa Rica 3-0 on Wednesday to make it two wins from two games for a squad packed with MLS players during the Americans' annual January camp.
article.linkText: Agyemang scores again as U.S. tops Costa Rica

article.story:
The United States beat Costa Rica 3-0 on Wednesday to make it two wins from two games for a squad packed with MLS players during the Americans' annual January camp.

Vancouver Whitecaps striker Brian White opened the scoring for the U.S., beating Esteban Alvarado after Diego Luna played him into the area with an incisive through ball.

White, who turns 29 on Feb. 3, became the oldest American to score his first international goal since 29-year-old Matt Besler in 2016.

Mauricio Pochettino's side, fresh off a win over Venezuela in another friendly on Saturday, needed a big save from Zack Steffen late in the first half to preserve its 1-0 lead at the break.

Steffen came up big for the U.S. again in the second half, before Caden Clark and Patrick Agyemang scored a pair of stellar goals late in the match to put the game away.

"He performed really, really well," Pochettino said about Steffen. "A few saves were amazing. He showed the quality that he has as a keeper."

It was the second goal in as many games for Agyemang, who scored on his debut for the U.S. in the 3-0 win over Venezuela.

The result handed former Mexico coach Miguel Herrera a loss in his first match since taking over the Costa Rica job.

Pochettino will lead a full-strength U.S. side into action in March for the Concacaf Nations League finals, where the Americans will try to remain the only team to have ever won the competition since its inception in 2019.

"The inclusion of non-traditional players on the national team is very useful and provides new information about the real level of those players," Pochettino said after the match.

"They are players with talent, with the ability to understand and adapt quickly to different ideas. And I think that will help us in the future to make better decisions and to create a balanced squad for future competitions."

The U.S. opens play in the Nations League on March 20 against Panama at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.

Europe-based players will be available for the Nations League, one of just six training camps remaining with the top pool before players arrive ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

Steffen was among eight changes to the starting lineup from Saturday's win and defender Miles Robinson, among the three holdovers, made his first international start at right-back.

Central defenders Tim Ream and Walker Zimmerman were added to the lineup along with left-back DeJuan Jones, Luna in midfield, winger Indiana Vassilev and White. Ream and Zimmerman became the first pair of over-30 non-goalkeepers to start since Ream and Michael Bradley in 2019.

Information from The Associated Press was used in this recap.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! USA 1, Costa Rica 0. Brian White (USA) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Diego Luna with a through ball.
Santiago van der Putten (Costa Rica) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
keyEvents.shortText: Santiago van der Putten Yellow C

keyEvents.text: Miles Robinson (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half begins USA 1, Costa Rica 0.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Anthony Walker replaces Esteban Alvarado.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Orlando Sinclair replaces Josimar Méndez.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Allan Cruz replaces Rashir Parkins.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Kevin Espinoza replaces Santiago van der Putten.
Substitution, USA. Caden Clark replaces Benjamín Cremaschi.
Substitution, USA. Jack McGlynn replaces Brian Gutiérrez.
Substitution, USA. Matko Miljevic replaces Diego Luna.
Substitution, USA. Shaq Moore replaces Miles Robinson.
Fernán Faerron (Costa Rica) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Jack McGlynn (USA) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Anthony Hernández replaces Andy Rojas.
Substitution, USA. Max Arfsten replaces Indiana Vassilev.
Substitution, Costa Rica. Diego Campos replaces Creichel Pérez.
Matko Miljevic (USA) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, USA. Patrick Agyemang replaces Brian White.
Goal! USA 2, Costa Rica 0. Caden Clark (USA) left footed shot from outside the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Emeka Eneli with a cross.
Goal! USA 3, Costa Rica 0. Patrick Agyemang (USA) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by DeJuan Jones with a through ball.
Second Half ends, USA 3, Costa Rica 0.
